title: Mullin's Kent Coronation
theme: David Mullin leads from Royal Cinque Ports to the final green at Prince's — a wire-to-wire double, only made interesting by a late wobble and an Alex Baker meltdown at the 16th that fits a tournament-long pattern.
opening_hook: Open on Alex Baker standing on the 16th tee at Prince's in R4, Mullin's eight-shot Trophy lead whittled to something nervous — then a 10 lands on the card (b02) and the chase is over. Cue flashback to Deal, Sunday, four days earlier.
foreshadow: Mullin's quiet outright Trophy takeover at the 16th at Royal Cinque Ports in R1 (b38) — the only lead change that ever mattered.
Alex Baker's habit of compiling 10s on par-fours and -fives (b04, b09, b10, b11, b12) building to b02.
Jon Baker dropping to 4th on R1 H4 (b31) and never escaping (spoon arc).
Mullin's lead at 9 after R2, still 8 after R3 — the procession that flattered to deceive before R4.

competitions.name: Trophy (Stableford)
competitions.how: Mullin took outright lead at R1 H16 (b38) after drawing level at H11 (b41), powered by a 13-point burst across holes 9-12 (b16), and never gave it back. Margins of 3, 9, 8, 2 tell the story: a runaway through R2 (41 pts) and R3, then a wobble in R4 — Mullin's own quad at the 10th (b08) opened the door and Alex Baker walked through it with hot stretches (b22, b19) before slamming it shut himself with a 10 at the 16th (b02, b26). Final margin: 2.

competitions.name: Green Jacket (Gross)
competitions.how: Brief Alex Baker level-peg at R1 H6 (b42), Mullin drew level at H9 (b40) and was gone — gross rounds of 15, 14, 20, 19 (+68 total). The Jacket race was effectively over by R2, when the lead ballooned to 12. Jon Baker — yes, that Jon Baker — was runner-up at +75, seven adrift (b03).

competitions.name: Wooden Spoon
competitions.how: Sealed on Day One, Hole Four. Jon dropped to 4th at R1 H4 (b31) with a bogey at the par-three and stayed welded to the bottom for 68 holes. Round scores of 29, 29, 28 confirmed it; a face-saving 39 in R4 (with a 14-point burst at 12-15, b06) flattered the corpse before a quad at the 18th (b14) provided fitting last rites. 125 points total (b05). Curiously, the same player also finished second for the Jacket.

headline_candidates: Mullin Plants the Flag at Deal
Royal Cinque Ports, Royal Procession Begins
rounds.chosen_headline: Mullin Plants the Flag at Deal
rounds.angle: Establish the wire-to-wire: Mullin's 40-point opener at brutal Royal Cinque Ports, the H9 gross level-peg and H16 outright Stableford takeover, while Alex Baker burns six shots at 3-5 and posts a 10 at the 16th. Jon Baker drops to 4th at H4 — and stays there for the duration.

The procession: 41 points on the Romney Marsh, lead stretched to 9 (Trophy) and 12 (Jacket). Two Alex Baker quads (b10, b11) at 5 and 8 do the damage; Mullin twice steadies himself with birdies after rough patches (b20, b21).

Mullin survives his own quad at the 8th (b07) by being far stronger on the back nine — a 10-point split (b18) capped by a 13-point closing flourish (b17). Alex Baker bleeds early (b37) and quads the 10th (b12); Jon Baker quads the 13th (b13) and Gregg Williams collapses at 12 (b28). Lead still 8.

The only real drama. Mullin's quad at the 10th (b08) plus Alex Baker's flying start (b22) and 11-point 13-15 surge (b19) cut the lead to genuinely tight — and then Alex makes a 10 at the 16th (b02, b26), echoing his R1 10 at the same numbered hole. Jon Baker's brief 14-point burst (b06) gives way to a closing quad at 18 (b14). Mullin wins by 2.

players.arc: Wire-to-wire winner of both Trophy and Jacket; never relinquished either lead from R1 H16 onward, weathering his own quads at the 8th (R3) and 10th (R4) to win the Trophy by 2 and the Jacket by 7.

The nearly man and the meltdown specialist: posted 10s in R1 and R4 (both at the 16th), quads at the 5th, 8th and 10th, yet kept clawing back with hot stretches in R4 before the decisive blow-up sealed Mullin's win.

The schizophrenic scorecard: Wooden Spoon from R1 H4 to the final green, yet somehow runner-up for the Green Jacket — a 39-point R4 (including a 14-point burst at 12-15) couldn't disguise three openers of 29, 29, 28.
Mid-pack throughout: a 9 at the 3rd in R1, a tidy 9-point stretch in R3 undone immediately by a triple bogey at the 12th.

venue_notes: Frame as TEG's 2nd visit to Kent, November 2021. Use the links setting throughout: Royal Cinque Ports as a 'formidable championship links' for the R1 scene-setter (and note its Open Championship pedigree); Littlestone as the 'remote Romney Marshes' Purves/Braid/MacKenzie test for R2; Prince's Shore/Dunes for the R3-R4 finale, leaning on its status as the strongest routing and the only repeat visit (3rd TEG round there) — fitting that the tournament was decided on familiar coastal ground.
